title: Why are Angels vulnerable to N mines
display_title: Why are Angels vulnerable to N mines
wikitext:
[]: Why are Angels vulnerable to N² mines?             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Fri Dec 05, 2008 9:57 pm N² mines were used with some success against Sachiel and the Twins. What's up with that?

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Dec 05, 2008 10:03 pm An AT Field is only so strong. It is not all powerful. Why even ask?

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Fri Dec 05, 2008 10:14 pm Because it is stated somewhere in the first episode that AT Fields are basically impervious to conventional weapons.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Dec 05, 2008 10:15 pm I do not believe that an N^2 mine is a "conventional" weapon.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Dec 05, 2008 10:29 pm Ramiel's AT Field seems to have been pierced with a positron canon, it took a lot of power, but it happened. Also: Episode 01 wrote: OFFICER A: The tank battalion's been annihilated. Guided missiles and artillery have no effect either. OFFICER B: It's no good! We'll never get anywhere with the firepower we've got! FUYUTSUKI: Is it an AT field after all? IKARI: Yes. Conventional weapons are useless against Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Fri Dec 05, 2008 10:43 pm Remember "We'll have to redraw the map again"? It's because N^2 weapons do a LOT of damage. I think the Evapedia says they do the damage of a nuclear blast without the nuclear fallout. Basically, like the others said, they're just really big weapons, and AT Fields aren't impenetrable. Heck, it took three AT fields just to SUPPORT Sahaquiel.

[]:             Quote Post by Ark » Sat Dec 06, 2008 6:02 am Plot convenience.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Dec 06, 2008 10:25 am they're the most powerful human weapon ever made (not an Eva) but they can only moderately hurt some of the WEAKER Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sat Dec 06, 2008 12:46 pm It's a bloody big bomb. An AT field can only stop so much energy. The positron cannon blasted through with a few hundred highly focused Gigawatts behind it. Using the megaton equivelant scale, assuming the Positron Cannon has about a 200GW behind it A reasonable figure given Tokyo Electric supplies about 63.4, I can't find statistics for the whole of Japan. It is firing for one second (For Maths), we get 200GJ of energy required to break an AT field. (The energy of course is focused to a point.) It's probably a bit more or less, but that makes a bit of sense to me. Anyone who can come with the a good estimate of total Japanese electrical demand can correct that. So then, lets get the megaton equivelant of that: 1 megaton - 4.184×10^15 J So then, One Ton of TNT has an energy of 4.184x10^9 Joules. That's twice the bang of the Positron Cannon at about 2x10^9, or half a ton of TNT. Even upscaling to half a terrawatt of power... a plausible enough figure for power generation... it still pretty much equal footing with a ton and a bit of TNT. That's a mid-sized truckbomb. The sort that level Federal Buildings. Little Boy, gave out about 13-15kt, which is about 5.8x10^13 Joules. That is 1945 technology. It'd also be a good ballpark equivelant for the bomb dropped on Sachiel, based on relative effects. But, now here's the thing, the blast energy is spread over a much larger area than the energy of the positron beam. It's like pushing down on the point of a thumbtack, compared to the head...Or hitting someone with the end of a baseball bat, compared to the pointy end of a knife. The blast from the N2 doesn't concentrate that energy into a small enough space. It acts over a much larger (several orders of magnitude even) surface area of the AT field. So the baseball bat will stun you... and it stuns the angels, but it's the knife that kills 'em So why didn't they used a larger Hydrogen bomb? Or several? A 1950's vintage 50 Megaton Hydrogen bomb gives about 5.4 yottawatts for about 29 nanoseconds. Or 2.09x10^17 Joules. And the scientists who built that bomb could easily have made it twice as powerful? So why not just drop one or ten of those badboys on an Angel? Or 100 N2's Because, unfortunately, people live under them. People have to continue to live under them. And the fallout from that much atomic fire would turn most of eastern Asia into an atomic wasteland. That, and the angels are protected by the strongest armour of all... Plot Armour.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Dec 06, 2008 4:43 pm That and there is a limited supply of N2s--- only 992 left by ep16. Also, too much bang and they risk taking out Lilith as well, who probably doesn't even have an active ATF at all... most likely the real target of the NERV self destruct system.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sat Dec 06, 2008 6:52 pm But, there's more than enough of ye old family Atomics floating around for when the N2's run out. Even after they were 'banned' by the Valentine treaty. Maggotmaster was 'banned' and he's still around here plenty. I wonder if the self destruct device itself in NERV HQ wouldn't be thermonuclear? I don't know where I read it, but somewhere said N2's were antimatter based. The preproduction material calls them P-bombs... that's either Proton, or Positronium. Positronium being a form of antimatter. It would take the annihilation of 1kg matter/anti-matter to match the largest explosive thermonuclear yield. Why am I waffling on this tangeant. I dunno. As a fanfic plotline, it would make one hell of a Nero order. Instead of the invasion in EoE... turn the whole lot into a smoking crater.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sat Dec 06, 2008 8:19 pm I'm quite surprised people don't agree here that AT fields are powerful, but with enough energy, can be broken. That's really all there is too it.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Dec 06, 2008 8:20 pm A more general question might be why they even bothered with the pallet riffles and other conventional projectiles since they are obviously pointless against all but the most pathetic angel, and they should have anticipated that. If they don't work from the automated defenses or JSSDF forces, why it work when the eva does the aiming?

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sat Dec 06, 2008 8:21 pm ^ I'd say since Evas have their own AT fields to neutralize the angels, a pallet rifle would be a decent mid range weapon to smack the angel with once its AT field is down.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Dec 06, 2008 8:31 pm They do that and the rifles are still absolutely useless against every single one of them except Matarael, who is only notable for being the pathetic exception. Hell, if it wasn't for the blackout he probably would have been cut down in a hail of automated gunfire before the evas even launched.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sat Dec 06, 2008 8:43 pm I'm thinking the Evas' AT fields in the cases where the rifle doesn't work aren't strong enough to neutralize the angels' fields. The prog knife seems to be the only good weapon I've seen used in the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Sat Dec 06, 2008 11:35 pm Weren't Gendo and Fuyutsuki surprised in Episode 1 that Sachiel had an AT field? At the very least, didn't they say something like "so it IS an AT field after all"? If that's the case, the people in charge of producing the Eva stuff, such as weaponry, probably were told to be prepared in case conventional weapons COULD work. Besides, it looks cool unloading a gatling gun and crushing cars with the spent shells.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 07, 2008 1:31 am Ark wrote: Plot convenience.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sun Dec 07, 2008 7:32 am I think the first few hits from the pallet rifle weakened Shamshel's AT Field by a very slight degree, after which Shamshel simply moved out of the way. Which was the point of Misato going "Baka! The enemy's hidden by the smokescreen!" Or words to that effect.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Dec 07, 2008 10:42 pm V wrote: they're the most powerful human weapon ever made (not an Eva) but they can only moderately hurt some of the WEAKER Angels.
